description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, dark stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly black surface mottled with lighter gray and off-white patches. The stone's surface is rough and uneven, with visible chips and natural weathering, giving it a raw, unpolished appearance. One side features a slightly concave indentation, while the opposite side is more convex and broad. There is a visible marking or label, 'HL-329', painted in white on one face, suggesting it may have been cataloged or used for identification. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall form is asymmetrical, with a tapering lower end and a broader, more irregular upper section.
description: A rough, irregular black stone with mottled gray and white patches, featuring a catalog label and a rugged, weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broadest face with mottled black, gray, and white coloration and a rugged, uneven surface.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the stone's thin profile and angular edges, highlighting its irregular thickness.
description_view_2: Back view displays the catalog label 'HL-329' and a more convex, less mottled surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows a narrow, elongated profile with visible surface texture and color variation.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as an obstacle barrier
attribute_pattern: Mottled pattern with irregular patches of gray and off-white on a predominantly black surface; no artificial carvings or regular veining.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible chips and surface irregularities, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth, man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; could be partially surrounded by soil or vegetation in parks.
